title: The noticeboard
premise: The city's last messages, layered over each other.

stages.narration: A public board, papered three deep with the last weeks of the old world: missing faces, meeting times that never came, a phone number ringed twice in biro. Someone has written across all of it, later, in heavy marker: WE WENT NORTH.

choices.label: Read them all — learn the shape of what happened here
Pocket the ringed number in case it ever matters
Move on; you've read enough of these

notes: city-voices cluster. Reading spends time for a small steadying under the grief; the number is a hook that sits as unease; leaving is free but flat. FR-ENC-01/02 ambient.
